Category: Activation: Channel syndication & endpoint registry
Feature: Channel adapter for Amazon Vendor Central (1P)
Description: Provide a configurable adapter that maps internal product model to Amazon Vendor Central (1P) requirements (attributes, allowed values, media constraints), generates channel-ready payloads, and supports scheduled publish as well as on-demand push.
Example vendors: Salsify; Syndigo; PIMworks; Icecat (varies by endpoint)
Why this is differentiating: Channel-specific packaging of content with validations, mapping, and automated delivery is a key differentiator vs basic 'export'.
Primary user role: Activation Manager; eCommerce Ops; Integration Engineer
UI surface: Admin UI + Background Jobs + API
Data entities: Channel, Channel Spec, Attribute Mapping, Media Rule, Publish Job, Publish Log
Closest Frappe modules: Frappe DocType + Workflow + REST API (custom)
Non-functional notes: Must support incremental publish, retries, idempotency, and rate-limit handling per endpoint.
Security/Compliance notes: Store endpoint credentials in encrypted secrets store; per-channel permission scopes.
Acceptance criteria: Admin can configure mappings; system validates against Amazon Vendor Central (1P) rules; publish job produces a payload and records success/failure with actionable errors.

Category: AI: enrichment automation and generative content ops
Feature: Generate product descriptions from structured attributes
Description: Provide Generate product descriptions from structured attributes as an automated capability with configurable inputs/outputs, confidence scoring, and optional human approval before committing changes to the canonical record.
Example vendors: Salsify (AI automation); Bluestone (AI Enrich); PIMworks (AI enrichment); inRiver (AI-powered enrichment)
Why this is differentiating: AI-driven enrichment and governance of generated content is a differentiator, especially at scale.
Primary user role: Catalog Manager; Content Writer; Data Steward
UI surface: Bulk Jobs + Review Queue + Product Editor Assist
Data entities: AIJob, AISuggestion, PromptTemplate, GlossaryTerm, ApprovalTask
Integration touchpoints: LLM provider APIs; translation engines; media processing
Closest Frappe modules: Frappe File; Website/Portal; REST API; Report Builder
Non-functional notes: Batch processing; idempotency; cost controls; caching; observability for job failures.
Security/Compliance notes: Do not send sensitive partner data to LLMs; redact secrets; store prompts/outputs with access controls.
Acceptance criteria: Users can run AI job on a product set, review suggestions, approve/reject, and see audit logs including model and prompt version.

Example vendors: inRiver Media Suite; Pimberly (integrated DAM); Bluestone (DAM); Pimcore (DAM/PIM)
Why this is differentiating: Rich DAM capabilities (renditions, rights, 3D, CDN) differentiate advanced PIM/PXM tools from basic file attachments.
Primary user role: Digital Asset Manager; Content Ops
UI surface: Asset Library + Product Media Tab
Data entities: Asset, AssetVersion, Rendition, Rights, AssetUsage
Closest Frappe modules: Frappe File; Website/Portal; REST API; Report Builder
Non-functional notes: Large file support; resumable uploads; virus scanning; CDN friendly URLs.
Security/Compliance notes: Signed URLs; fine-grained permissions; PII-free metadata; license compliance.
Acceptance criteria: Assets can be uploaded, tagged, versioned, transformed into renditions, linked to products, and published as channel-specific sets.

Acceptance criteria: Partner can access a portal, search products, download assets, and see only permitted assortment; all access is logged.
